DEFENDANTS PRINCIPAL BUILDERS, INC., SCOTT REAY and JON-MICHAEL JOHNSON'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Cross-Complaint is denied. The Court may not adjudicate a portion of a cause of action. (CCP sec. 437c(f)(1).) Although the parties are apparently in agreement that Cross-Defendant Laurie Anderson owes Cross-Complainant the amount of a loan and approximately $200 in credit card expenses, the seventh cause of action also alleges that Ms. Anderson owes $3,565.38 for contributions to her health savings account and approximately $700 in credit card expenses. The motion for summary adjudication does not address these allegations and therefore the motion would not dispose of the entire cause of action. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Off Calendar Jul-31-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. STEPHEN SOMMERS & ATTY. ASHLEY BALTAZAR OF SOMMERS EMPLOYMENT LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 524-2860 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WILLIAM HAYS WEISSMAN & ATTY. LAURA E. CURTIS OF THE LITTLER MENDELSON, P.C., TEL. NO. (925) 927-4545 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC., SCOTT REAY, JON-MICHAEL JOHNSON and CHUCK BONECK's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Defendants fail to show that the DFEH charges are defective as a matter of law. "Administrative charges are to be construed liberally because they are often drafted by claimants without the assistance of counsel. Accordingly, '[i]t is sufficient that the EEOC be apprised, in general terms, of the alleged discriminatory parties and the alleged discriminatory acts.'" Nazir v. United Airlines, Inc. (2009) 178 Cal.App.4th 243, 266-267. In this case, the charges meet the requirements of Cal. Gov't Code sec. 12960 and 2 CCR sec. 10005. The demurrer is moot as to Mr. Boneck. The evidentiary objection was sustained by the court. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: John Stewart; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =(302/JKS)
